ptxt.intxrefpoff: Main Bearings, Crankshaft and Cylinder Block Removal and Installation

item.ptxt: Service main bearings are available in standard size and 0.25 mm undersize, and each of them has 5 kinds of bearings differing in tolerance.
Upper bearing half (1) has oil groove (2) as shown in figure.
Install this half with oil groove to cylinder block (3).
Lower bearing half does not have an oil groove.

servinfosub.title: Visual Inspection
servinfosub.ptxt: Check bearings for pitting, scratches, wear or damage.
If any defect is found, replace both upper and lower halves. Never replace either half without replacing the other half.

servinfosub.title: Main Bearing Clearance

attention3.ptxt: Do not rotate crankshaft while gauging plastic is installed.

servinfosub.ptxt: Check clearance using gauging plastic according to the following procedure.

s1.ptxt: Remove main bearing caps.
Clean bearings and main journals.
Place a piece of gauging plastic (1) the full width of bearing (parallel to crankshaft) on journal, avoiding oil hole.
Install main bearing cap.
After three minutes, remove main bearing cap and using scale (2) on gauging plastic envelope (1), measure gauging plastic width at its widest point.
If measured clearance is less than standard or more than service limit, replace main bearings with new ones referring to Selection of Main Bearing and recheck main bearing clearance.
If measured clearance with new bearing is out of standard, select other bearing according to the following procedure and recheck main bearing clearance.

item.ptxt: Replace bearing with one size thicker or regrind crankshaft journal to undersize and use 0.25 mm undersize bearing.
Replace bearing with one size thinner or regrind crankshaft journal to undersize and use 0.25 mm undersize bearing.

topic.title: Standard size bearing
topic.ptxt: If engine is under one of the following conditions, select a new standard bearing as follows, and install it.

item.ptxt: Bearing clearance is not as specified.
Crankshaft or cylinder block is replaced.

s1.ptxt: Check journal diameter. As shown in figure, crank web No.4 has stamped numbers.
Six kinds of numbers (“1” through “6”) represent the following journal diameters.
Stamped numbers on crank web No.4 represent diameters of journal indicated with an arrow in figure respectively.
For example stamped number “1” indicates that corresponding journal diameter is 45.015 – 45.018 mm (1.7722 – 1.7723 in.).
Check bearing cap bore diameter without bearing. On mating surface of cylinder block, five alphabets are stamped as shown in figure (1).
Six kinds of alphabets (“A” through “F”) or numbers (“1” through “6”) represent the following cap bore diameters.
Stamped alphabets or numbers on cylinder block represent bearing cap bore diameter marked with an arrow in figure respectively.
For example, stamped “A” or “1” indicates that corresponding bearing cap bore diameter is 49.000 – 49.003 mm (1.92913 – 1.92925 in.).
There are 5 kinds of standard bearings differing in thickness. To distinguish them, they are painted in the following colors at the position as indicated in figure.
Each color indicated the following thickness at the center of bearing.

spec.title: Standard size crankshaft main bearing thickness

s1.ptxt: From number stamped on crank web No.4 and alphabets stamped on cylinder block, determine new standard bearing to be installed to journal, by referring to the table shown.
For example, if number stamped on crank web No.4 is “1” and alphabet stamped on cylinder block is “B”, install a new standard bearing painted in “Pink” to its journal.

spec.title: Main bearing cross – reference selection table (standard size)

entry.ptxt: Number stamped on crank web No.4
Alphabet or number stamped on cylinder block

s1.ptxt: Using scale (2) and gauging plastic (1), check bearing clearance with newly selected standard bearing.
If clearance still exceeds its limit, use next thicker bearing and recheck clearance, referring to Main Bearing Clearance .

item.ptxt: 0.25 mm undersize bearing is available, in five kinds varying in thickness.
To distinguish them, each bearing is painted in the following colors at such position as indicated in figure.
Each color represents the following thickness at the center of bearing.

spec.title: Undersize of crankshaft main bearing thickness

item.ptxt: If necessary, regrind crankshaft journal and select undersize bearing to use with it as follows.

s1.ptxt: Regrind journal to the following finished diameter.
Using micrometer, measure regrind journal diameter.
Measurement should be taken in two directions perpendicular to each other in order to check for out-of-roundness.
Using journal diameter measured above and alphabets stamped on cylinder block, select an undersize bearing by referring to the following table.
Check bearing clearance with newly selected undersize bearing.

spec.title: Main bearing cross – reference table (under size)

entry.ptxt: Alphabet or number stamped on cylinder block

servinfosub.title: Main Bearing Cap Bolt
servinfosub.ptxt: Measure each thread diameter at specified points on main bearing cap bolts (1) using a micrometer (3).
If it exceeds limit, replace main bearing cap bolt with new one.

spec.title: Main bearing cap bolt diameter measurement points
Main bearing cap bolt diameter difference
